mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor demand and income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases month-over-month, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is less than 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated CPI level (CPIAUCSL above 200) triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, which raises the discount rate on equity cash flows and depresses stock prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 200, the median 20-day SPX return is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over all historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy (FEDFUNDS above 4.5%) and declining industrial production (INDPRO month-over-month decrease) jointly signals a recessionary impulse, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In instances where both conditions hold simultaneously, the average 60-day SPX return is not negative, or the rank IC of the interaction signal is below 0.04 over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 4.5%, positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand that feeds into corporate profits and lifts equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 4.5 and RSAFS grows month-over-month, the 20-day SPX return is negative more than 45% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A jump in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market weakness that erodes consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses above 5.5, the proportion of negative 60-day SPX returns is less than 60%, or the average return is not negative over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: When RSAFS increases from the prior month, the proportion of positive 5-day SPX returns is less than 52% over any 2-year period, or the average return is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of falling housing starts (HOUST month-over-month decline) and elevated interest rates (FEDFUNDS above 4.0%) indicates a severe housing contraction that propagates to broader economic weakness, depressing S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST declines and FEDFUNDS > 4.0, the 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the information coefficient of the combined signal is below 0.03 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) is positively associated with equity returns only when retail sales (RSAFS) are also expanding, confirming that supply-side gains occur in a demand-rich environment that sustains earnings growth over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS YoY is positive and INDPRO increases month-over-month, the 20-day SPX return is not above its unconditional median, or the hit ratio is below 50% over a 5-year period.
